title: iOS Developr
description:

Optimize legacy code and improve performance, stability, and maintainability of the app.
Create and maintain technical documentation, including specifications and application details.
Review peer code, provide feedback, and ensure adherence to coding standards and security practices.
Monitor app performance, tune memory usage, and use tools such as Instruments for debugging.
Collaborate across departments and ensure consistent communication regarding project status and issues.
Support and mentor junior engineers; contribute to bootcamp sessions and technical knowledge sharing.
Stay updated on emerging trends and tools in mobile development and explore new technologies for process improvement.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related field.
3–5 years of proven experience in
iOS development
using
Swift
and
Objective-C
.
Solid understanding of
MVP/MVVM
and
Clean Architecture
.
Experience with
Domain-Driven Development
.
Proficient with
Auto Layout
and programmatic UI building.

Architecture: MVVM, MVP, Clean Architecture
Frameworks: UIKit, Core Data, Cocoa Touch, Core Animation, Core Graphics

title: Business Analyst
description:

Prepare comprehensive documentation including Business Requirement Documents (BRD), Functional Specification Documents (FSD), user stories, workflows, and API documentation.

Perform User Acceptance Testing (UAT) to validate features and ensure high product quality before release.
Track agent performance, booking trends, and identify issues related to payment failures and user drop-offs.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including developers, designers, QA, and business leads for timely project execution.
Continuously improve processes and solutions based on feedback, performance metrics, and user behavior data.
Bachelor’s degree in Business Management, Information Technology, or a related field.
Minimum 3 to 4 years of professional experience in a similar business analysis role, preferably in the travel or online booking industry.
Strong understanding of Global Distribution Systems (GDS) like Sabre, Amadeus, or similar.
Proficient in data analysis, dashboarding, and reporting using tools such as Excel, Power BI, or Google Data Studio.
Hands-on experience with requirement documentation including BRD, FSD, user stories, and workflows.
Familiarity with API integration flows, bug tracking, and enhancement documentation.
Good understanding of payment gateways, settlement systems, and transaction reconciliation.

title: Business Analyst
location: F1Soft Tower, Pulchowk, Lalitpur District, Nepal
description:
Requirement Identification & Use Case Exploration:
Proactively explore and analyze complex datasets to identify potential business opportunities, new use cases, and unmet user needs. Utilize data analytics tools and methodologies to drive evidence-based requirement discovery.
Data Analysis :
Define detailed data requirements, create data flow diagrams, specify source-to-target mappings, and collaborate with Data Engineers and Data Scientists on logical data modeling (dimensional or relational) to support analytics initiatives requirements.
Detailed Requirement Analysis & Documentation:
Conduct thorough stakeholder interviews and workshops. Translate high-level business needs into clear, concise, and detailed functional and non-functional requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria.
Product Ownership & Scrum Management:
Act as the primary Product Owner for a cross-functional Scrum team. Define, prioritize, and manage the team’s backlog. Lead Scrum ceremonies including sprint planning, review, and retrospectives. Ensure alignment between the development team's output and the strategic product roadmap.
Backlog Management & Prioritization:
Maintain a healthy, refined, and prioritized Sprint backlog. Ensure transparency and understanding of backlog items across the team and stakeholders. Continuously groom the backlog to ensure items are ‘Ready’ for development sprints.
Research and domain understanding:
Conduct both qualitative and quantitative research in each business domain to discover business use cases, data availability and maintenance. Qualitative research encompasses exploring government regulation and directives, identifying macroeconomic trends from news, conducting in person interviews and catch-up sessions, studying related theories and journals etc. Furthermore, quantitative research encompasses exploratory data analysis on sample data, creating data and business logics to integrate in product modules, etc.
Stakeholder Communication & Alignment:
Serve as the key liaison between business stakeholders, data science teams, and the engineering team. Ensure all parties have a shared understanding of product vision, requirements, and progress.
Solution Validation & UAT Support:
Collaborate with QA teams to define test strategies. Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) processes and validate that delivered solutions meet the original business objectives and quality standards.
Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field such as IT, Computer Science, Data Science Statistics, and IT Management. Master Degree is a plus.
Minimum of 2- years of experience as a Business Analyst, Product Owner, or similar role within a software development industry.
Proven experience working in an Agile/Scrum environment, ideally as a certified Scrum Product Owner (CSPO) or Professional Scrum Product Owner (PSPO) or Professional Business Analyst.
Experience working specifically within a data analytics, AI, or data product company is highly desirable.
Analytical and problem-solving skills, with a strong ability to interpret complex data and derive actionable insights from the data to fit with buisness solutions.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with an ability to interpret complex data.
Proficiency with requirement management tools (e.g., Jira, Confluence).
Understanding of SQL, Python and data visualization tools (e.g. Power BI) is a major plus.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, capable of communicating effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.
